headline: Survey at 36% completion; strong regional spread but significant scale-up momentum needed.
collection_status: 481 of 1,350 responses (35.6%) collected across all 10 regions, with LINDI (89), MARA (78), and TANGA (64) leading. Collection pace supports on-time delivery if momentum maintained. Geographic distribution proportional but uneven—RUKWA at only 19 responses requires targeted push.
key_findings: Teacher respondents dominate (65% of sample): 195 KF lower-primary, 115 head teachers, 65 KF upper-primary—core implementer voice is strong.
Expansion priority is clear: 175 stakeholders prioritize expanding KF (36% of priorities), vs. 76 for strengthening existing—demand signal favors growth over consolidation.
Partner satisfaction healthy (4.4/5) but scale readiness lags significantly (2.8/5)—indicates confidence in approach but concern about operationalizing expansion.
Feedback reveals three recurring themes: sustained teacher incentives, digital monitoring systems, and government policy integration for sustainability.
flags: Scale readiness gap (2.8/5) suggests infrastructure, staffing, or systems constraints not yet visible in satisfaction scores—validate capacity assumptions before acceleration.
Low representation of regional/district education officials (REO, ARC, DEO, REO = 16 responses, 3.3%)—critical for policy buy-in; increase senior government sampling.
Research priority ranked lowest (9 responses, 1.9%)—risk of evidence gaps if expansion outpaces learning; embed M&E requirement in growth plans.
recommendation: Immediately audit scale readiness barriers (staffing, systems, partner capacity) against the 175-stakeholder expansion demand. Simultaneously intensify sampling of government education officials (target 50+ more responses) to ensure policy alignment before proposing institutional integration to government.
